title: Revelation 7
intro: Rev 7 is the **sealing chapter** — the interlude between the sixth and seventh seals that answers Rev 6:17's question ("_who shall be able to stand?_") by revealing the 144,000 sealed servants of God + the great multitude. Smith and Bohr converge on **every major identification**: the four angels holding the four winds = the providential restraint of the destroying judgments until the sealing work is complete; the sealing angel ascending from the east = the great sealing message of the last generation; the seal of the living God in the foreheads = the Sabbath as the great test of loyalty to the Creator at the close of probation; the 144,000 = the last-generation faithful, organized as **spiritual Israel** (not literal Jewish lineage), drawn from "_every nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people_"; the great multitude before the throne = the redeemed of all ages, including the 144,000 as a special class within them. The principal divergence (very soft) is on the **relation of the 144,000 to the great multitude**: Smith allows the 144,000 to be a distinct sub-class within the larger multitude (the last-generation faithful who pass through the time of trouble without seeing death), while Bohr collapses the two groups so that "_the 144,000 and the great multitude are the same group of end-time saints viewed from two different angles — the 144,000 as the numbered, ordered, militarily-organized army of God on earth, and the great multitude as the same group viewed in their post-translation glorified state in heaven_" (Bohr, paraphrasing Stefanovic and Paulien). This is a soft refinement, not a structural divergence — Smith's reading is large enough to accommodate the Bohr identification as a possibility. **No hard Miller-rule violations in this chapter.** Source: Bohr, _Revelation's Seven Seals: Studies in Revelation 4-8_, Lesson 15 (pp. 281-313).

verses.text: And after these things I saw four angels standing on the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, that the wind should not blow on the earth, nor on the sea, nor on any tree.

pioneerReadings.html: **Same identification as Smith — the four angels are God's restraining agents holding back the destroying winds until Jesus' high-priestly work in the most holy place is finished and the sealing of the 144,000 is complete.** EGW had this in vision Jan 5, 1849: "_I saw that the four angels would hold the four winds until Jesus' work was done in the sanctuary, and then will come the seven last plagues_" (EW 36.2). The vision is repeated and expanded with the cry "_Hold! Hold! Hold! Hold! until the servants of God are sealed in their foreheads_" (EW 38.1). The angel "_with a commission from Jesus, swiftly flying to the four angels who had a work to do on the earth_" (EW 38.1) gives the source for the sealing-angel identification at v.2.

**Four winds = strife, commotion, and political/religious destroying judgments; four angels holding them = the providential restraint of those judgments until the sealing work is complete.** "**Winds in prophecy denote political commotion and strife (cf. Jer 25:31-33, 49:36-37; Dan 7:2). The four angels are the providential agents commissioned to restrain those destroying winds until the sealing work of the last generation has been completed. The text plainly teaches that there is an interval between the cosmic signs of the sixth seal (Rev 6:12-13) and the great day of wrath (Rev 6:14-17) — an interval in which the sealing work of the third angel's message (Rev 14:9-12) goes forward, and at the close of which the four winds are loosed for the seven last plagues**" (DAR 435.1-437.1, condensed paraphrase).

**Convergent with Smith — the four winds are the seven last plagues held back until the sealing message is complete.** "_The wrath of God mentioned by the third angel is the seven last plagues. 'In them is filled up the wrath of God,' Revelation 15:1. The four angels [Revelation 7:1,] will hold the four winds, and the vials of God's wrath will not be poured out until the saints hear the 'loud voice' of the third angel, and are sealed with the seal of the living God_" (TTAM 14.2). White ties the four-angels-holding-winds directly to the third angel's message and the seven last plagues, the same chain Smith later canonizes.

**Convergent with Smith — four angels = providential restrainers of political strife; the four-angel station is historically anchored in 1848.** "_The four angels had been stationed on the earth to hold the winds of strife till the servants of God could be sealed_" (SSP 132.1). Haskell adds a sharp historical marker absent from Smith: "_In the year 1848, the four angels of the seventh chapter of Revelation took their stand upon the four corners of the earth, to hold the winds of strife until the servants of God were sealed_" (SSP 247.1). The 1848 dating ties the verse to the simultaneous sudden European calm following the revolutions of that year (SSP 131-132) AND to the Sabbatarian-Adventist sealing movement that crystallized at the Sabbath conferences of the same year.

modernReadings.html: **Same identification — four winds = the destroying judgments held back by God's providential agents until the sealing of the 144,000 is complete.** Bohr cross-links to **Daniel 7:2** ("_the four winds of the heaven strove upon the great sea_") and **Jeremiah 49:36** ("_upon Elam will I bring the four winds from the four quarters of heaven, and will scatter them toward all those winds_") as the controlling Old Testament parallels. The interval-between-cosmic-signs-and-great-day-of-wrath is the same Bohr develops in his two-stage sixth-seal reading.

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: No violation. Convergent. Exemplary Rule-V cross-links (Dan 7:2, Jer 49:36).

verses.text: And I saw another angel ascending from the east, having the seal of the living God: and he cried with a loud voice to the four angels, to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea,

pioneerReadings.html: **Same identification as Smith — the sealing angel is the divinely-commissioned messenger restraining the four destroying angels until the servants of God receive the seal of the living God; the seal itself is the Sabbath of the fourth commandment.** "_Another angel was commissioned to fly swiftly to the four angels and bid them hold, until the servants of God were sealed with the seal of the living God in their foreheads_" (EW 38.2). The seal-as-Sabbath identification is anchored in GC: "_The seal of God's law is found in the fourth commandment. This only, of all the ten, brings to view both the name and the title of the Lawgiver. It declares Him to be the Creator of the heavens and the earth, and thus shows His claim to reverence and worship above all others_" (GC 452.1, applying Isa 8:16). The forehead-sealing is moral and internal: "_it is not any seal or mark that can be seen, but a settling into the truth, both intellectually and spiritually, so they cannot be moved_" (4BC 1161.6 / MS 173, 1902).

**The sealing angel ascending from the east (Greek anatolē, the rising of the sun) represents the great sealing message of the last generation — the message that brings the Sabbath truth to light and applies the seal of the living God to the foreheads of the 144,000.** "**The sealing angel comes from the east, the place of light, the place of the sun's rising. He is not a literal angel but a symbolic figure representing the body of messengers who proclaim the great Sabbath-sealing message of the third-angel hour. The seal of the living God is the Sabbath of the fourth commandment — the only commandment in the Decalogue that contains all three elements of an official seal: the name of the lawgiver (the LORD thy God), his title (he that made), and his territory (heaven and earth, the sea, and all that in them is). Ex 20:8-11**" (DAR 437.1-438.1, condensed paraphrase; Smith develops the Sabbath-as-seal argument extensively at DAR 438-444).

**Convergent with Smith — the sealing angel is the body of messengers delivering the third angel's message; the seal of the living God is the Sabbath of the fourth commandment.** "_This angel delivers the last message of mercy to the scattered flock; therefore, it is the sealing message_" (TTAM 15.1). White ties the sealing-angel to the man-with-the-writer's-inkhorn of Ezek 9:2-4 (TTAM 15.1) — exactly the OT type Smith cites — and then identifies the two opposing marks: "_The living God instituted, sanctified and blessed the seventh day in Eden, and with an audible voice from Mount Sinai decreed that it should be observed as holy time. The beast decreed that the first day of the week should be holy time. Here are seen the two opposite marks. Both are conspicuous, in the forehead, which denotes a prominent profession_" (TTAM 15.3). This 1850 essay is one of the earliest formal expositions of the Sabbath-as-seal doctrine.

**Convergent with Smith — sealing angel = the body of end-time messengers proclaiming the Sabbath-sealing message; the seal contains the three legal-document elements (name, title, territory) of the fourth commandment.** "_The use of the word seal directs the mind to a legal document. When a ruler's seal is attached to a legal paper, that seal contains the name of the one in authority, his right to rule, and the territory over which he rules. These features are all made prominent in the seal contained in the law of God_" (SSP 135.1). This is the exact three-elements-of-a-seal argument that becomes the standard Adventist proof — and Haskell traces it back through Bates and J. White to its 1840s formulation. The east-ascending angel cried "_Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God_" (SSP 132.2 quoting v.3).

**Convergent with Smith — the angel ascending from the east is the third angel's Sabbath-sealing message, historically anchored to 1848.** "_In Revelation 7:2 the work of preparing a people to stand when the great day of God's wrath shall come is symbolized by an angel 'ascending from the east,' or, as some translate, 'like the rising of the sun.' Behold the dawning of the day—first appear the rays of light in the east; these blend into greater clearness until the sun's broad, distinct disk is seen_" (GSAM 464.1). Loughborough makes the anatolē / sunrise identification explicit and pairs it with the historical claim: "_Such indeed has been the progress of the third angel's message since 1848, when its component truths were brought into distinct form_" (GSAM 464.2). Like Haskell, Loughborough dates the start of the sealing work to 1848 (the publication of Bates' Sabbath tract + the Sabbath conferences).

modernReadings.html: **Same identification — the sealing angel = the body of end-time messengers proclaiming the great Sabbath-sealing message; the seal of the living God = the Sabbath of the fourth commandment.** Bohr's anatolē / east / sunrise identification is verbatim convergent with Smith. Bohr stresses the **forehead** as the seat of the conscience and the will (the seat of moral choice, where allegiance is determined) and the **Sabbath-vs.-mark-of-the-beast** contrast as the great loyalty-test of the final crisis (cross-link to Rev 14:9-12, the third angel's message; pp. 285-292).

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: No violation. Convergent. The Sabbath-as-seal Rule-V identification (Ex 20:8-11 → Isa 8:16 → Rev 7:2-3) is exemplary.

verses.text: Saying, Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their foreheads.

pioneerReadings.html: **Convergent with Smith — the sealing in the foreheads is the Sabbath-test of loyalty drawn at the close of probation; it is a settling of the character into the truth, not a visible mark.** "_The Sabbath will be the great test of loyalty, for it is the point of truth especially controverted. When the final test shall be brought to bear upon men, then the line of distinction will be drawn between those who serve God and those who serve Him not.... While one class, by accepting the sign of submission to earthly powers, receive the mark of the beast, the other, choosing the token of allegiance to divine authority, receive the seal of God_" (GC 605.2). The internal-character side: the sealing is reached by "_an entire transformation in the character of the human agent, making him a new creature in Christ Jesus_" through the Spirit (6BC 1117.17). EGW also pointedly warns against speculative curiosity about who composes the 144,000 (7BC 918.5).

**The destroying judgments are held back until the sealing of God's servants is complete — the sealing is the great preparatory work that fits the 144,000 to stand without a mediator through the time of trouble and the seven last plagues.** Smith develops the Sabbath-sealing argument extensively across DAR 438-444, identifying the seal as the Sabbath of the fourth commandment via the three-elements-of-a-seal argument (name + title + territory).

**Convergent with Smith — the sealing message uses Ezek 9:2-4 as its OT type (man with the writer's inkhorn marking the sighing-and-crying ones), and the seal-vs.-mark distinction turns on Sabbath observance.** "_The third angel's sealing message, is represented by the man clothed with linen, with a writer's inkhorn by his side, [Ezekiel 9:2-4] marking the sighing and crying ones_" (TTAM 15.1). The reader is solemnly entreated: "_Think not that we were free from the mark of the beast when we left the churches. The second angel's message only brought us from that cage, where we are now free to hear the third, which if received and obeyed, will strip us from the last vestige of Popery, and seal us with the seal of the living God_" (TTAM 16.1). The two marks are mutually exclusive: Sabbath vs. Sunday.

**Convergent with Smith on the underlying Sabbath-as-seal framework, though Andrews develops it primarily through the change-of-the-law argument (Dan 7:25) rather than through the seal-of-Rev-7 directly.** "_It is a fact beyond dispute that the fourth commandment, some ages since, was changed from the rest-day of the Lord to the pagan festival of Sunday. This change was made in express contradiction of the Holy Scriptures.... It was accomplished by the great apostasy, which Daniel predicted should 'think to change times and laws.' This power is essentially the same as the beast which was to be worshiped by all the world_" (TMR 131.2). The mark-of-the-beast = Sunday observance, and by inverse implication the seal of God = the Sabbath which the papal power attempted to change (TMR 107.1-108.3). The sealing in the forehead is the open Sabbath-keeping profession; the mark in the forehead is open Sunday-keeping profession.

**Convergent with Smith — the closing work of earth IS the sealing work; the universe waits on its completion.** "_The closing work of the earth is the sealing of the servants of God. The universe is now waiting for that work to be completed. The only thing in heaven or earth that can hinder the work of God, is a lack of spirituality on the part of His chosen people_" (SSP 142.1). The seal and the counter-mark go on simultaneously: "_As Jehovah recognizes in the lives of His people the reflection of the law of His own eternal throne and the seal, His name or mark which is His Sabbath, so he who has, from the beginning, striven to_ [counter the work, places his counter-mark on his own people]" (SSP 259.1). The Sabbath-as-the-name-or-mark-which-is-His-Sabbath formula is the same three-elements identification.

**Convergent with Smith — the sealing message is the third angel's message specifically as it carries the Sabbath of the Lord; its prosperity since 1848 is the verifying mark of its divine origin.** "_It surely is not because the message is one that is pleasing to carnal hearts, that it has thus prospered; for it carries in its forefront the Sabbath of the Lord, the observance of which brings a heavy cross_" (GSAM 463.1). The Sabbath-sealing message progresses "_since 1848, when its component truths were brought into distinct form... the Lord is verifying his word to those who in this time, when his salvation is about to come, 'keep the Sabbath from polluting it'_" (GSAM 464.2, quoting Isa 56:1-2).

modernReadings.html: **Same identification — sealing = the Sabbath-and-character-perfection preparation of the 144,000 to stand in the time of trouble; convergent with Smith.** Bohr's Sabbath-seal argument is the same three-elements-of-a-seal argument with the same Ex 20:8-11 anchor.

verses.text: And I heard the number of them which were sealed: and there were sealed an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of Israel.

pioneerReadings.html: **Same identification as Smith — 144,000 = the last-generation faithful, translated alive at the second coming, the only company who can sing the new song because their experience (passing through Jacob's trouble without an intercessor) is unique.** "_These, having been translated from the earth, from among the living, are counted as 'the first fruits unto God and to the Lamb.' Revelation 15:2, 3; 14:1-5. 'These are they which came out of great tribulation;' they have passed through the time of trouble such as never was since there was a nation; they have endured the anguish of the time of Jacob's trouble; they have stood without an intercessor through the final outpouring of God's judgments_" (GC 648.3). The 144,000 are also explicitly identified as the living-saint company at the voice of God: "_The living saints, 144,000 in number, knew and understood the voice_" (EW 15.1). EGW deliberately refrains from speculative identification of WHO will compose the 144,000: "_It is not His will that they shall get into controversy over questions which will not help them spiritually, such as, Who is to compose the hundred and forty-four thousand_" (7BC 978.4).

**144,000 = the last-generation faithful drawn from spiritual Israel (not literal Jewish lineage); the twelve-tribes-of-Israel framework = the gospel-grafting structure of Romans 11 in which believing Gentiles are grafted into the olive tree of Israel and the unbelieving natural branches are broken off.** "**The 144,000 are not literal Jews of literal Jewish descent. The Christian dispensation knows no national distinction. The middle wall of partition is broken down (Eph 2:14). All believers, whether of Jewish or Gentile birth, are alike children of Abraham by faith (Gal 3:7, 29; Rom 4:11-12, 16). The 144,000 are sealed out of the spiritual Israel of the last generation — twelve thousand from each of the symbolic tribes, the number 144,000 expressing the completed-perfect organization of God's last-day army**" (DAR 446.1-451.1, condensed paraphrase; Smith argues the spiritual-Israel identification at length from Rom 11, James 1:1, Rev 21:12-14, and the Gal-3:29 grafting framework).

**MAJOR DIVERGENCE FROM SMITH — Miller reads the 144,000 as literal Jewish-Christian converts sealed at the close of the Jewish dispensation (1st century AD), NOT as last-generation spiritual Israel.** "_John first gives us an account of the number that were sealed in his day, out of all the tribes of Israel. They were sealed, as he tells us when he wrote, it being finished in the close of the Jewish dispensation. It being a complete number, 144,000, and therefore could be numbered; and as these were sealed at the close of that dispensation, so John now saw in vision a great number, which no man could number, sealed at the close of the Gentile dispensation_" (MWV2 187.1). This is the pre-Sabbatarian Millerite reading — strictly historicist, with the 144,000 as a finished 1st-century reality and the great multitude of v.9 as the still-future Gentile-dispensation harvest. The later Adventist consensus (Smith, EGW, J. White, Haskell, Loughborough) moves BOTH groups into the end-time. Miller's reading also implicitly treats the twelve tribes as literal Jewish tribes — a Rule-V failure later corrected via Rom 11 / Gal 3:29 / James 1:1.

**Convergent with Smith — 144,000 = end-time spiritual Israel, character-sealed, drawn from believers grafted into the olive tree contrary to nature (Rom 11 framework).** "_The number of the servants of our God is now being made up.... Character alone is the basis of the sealing work. The promise of the new earth was made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; but the literal descendants of these patriarchs, failed to develop a character which would place upon them a seal of the living God, and they were rejected as a nation. Then the Gentiles, like branches from a wild olive tree, were, contrary to nature, grafted into_ [the cultivated olive tree]" (SSP 136.2). This is the Rom 11 grafting argument that anchors Smith's spiritual-Israel reading, applied directly to v.4. Haskell explicitly rejects the literal-Jewish-lineage reading that Miller MWV2 187.1 had defended.

**Convergent with Smith on the twelve-tribes typology — Bates explicitly cross-references Rev 7:4 to the Numbers-1-and-2 wilderness camp organization, where Christ as High Priest bears "the twelve tribes of the House of Israel" on the breastplate of judgment.** "_Here his work ceased; Ministering and Mediating for the whole world forever; and he like his pattern in the type, entered the Most Holy Place, bearing upon his breast plate of Judgment the twelve tribes of the House of Israel. See Exodus 28:29, and Revelation 7:4_" (BP3 133.3). Bates supplies the typological substructure (Levi separated for the priesthood; Joseph counted as two tribes via Ephraim and Manasseh — BP3 129.7-15) that makes sense of the anomalous v.5-8 tribal list. The 144,000 are the antitypical wilderness camp, organized for the final atonement — sealed under the High Priest's most-holy ministration that began in 1844.

modernReadings.html: **Same identification — 144,000 = spiritual Israel of the last generation, drawn from every nation/kindred/tongue/people; convergent with Smith.** Bohr develops the same Rom-11 / Gal-3:29 grafting framework and adds the **square-of-12** symbolic-number argument: 144,000 = 12 (the patriarchs / OT covenant) × 12 (the apostles / NT covenant) × 1,000 (the symbolic-completeness multiplier) — the number expressing the **perfect organization of God's end-time army**, drawn from both covenants and from every nation (pp. 293-296).

verses.text: Of the tribe of Juda were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Reuben were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Gad were sealed twelve thousand.

pioneerReadings.html: **The list of twelve tribes is symbolic of the spiritual organization of the last-generation church.** Smith notes the **anomalous omissions** of Dan and Ephraim from the list (replaced by Levi and Joseph) and reads these omissions as deliberate symbolic markers — Dan and Ephraim being the tribes most associated with idolatry in OT history, omitted from the symbolic last-generation roll-call. Juda heads the list as the tribe of the Lion of Judah (Christ).

**Divergent from Smith — Miller treats the twelve tribes as LITERAL Jewish tribes sealed at the close of the Jewish dispensation, not as the symbolic spiritual-Israel structure.** "_John first gives us an account of the number that were sealed in his day, out of all the tribes of Israel.... It being a complete number, 144,000, and therefore could be numbered_" (MWV2 187.1). On this reading Judah, Reuben, and Gad are literal 1st-century tribal converts to Christianity, not symbols of any end-time grafting structure. The later Adventist consensus (Bates, Smith, Haskell) rejects the literal reading on Rule-V grounds (Rom 11, Gal 3:29, James 1:1) and identifies the v.5-8 list as a symbolic restructuring of the wilderness-camp organization (Num 1-2).

**Convergent with Smith on the symbolic-tribes framework — Bates supplies the Numbers-2 camp-of-Israel typology that Smith and Haskell later apply to Rev 7.** Bates lays out the four-sided wilderness camp: "_On the East towards the Sun's rising, Judah, Issachar and Zebulon: numbering 186,400 under the standard of the Lion. On the South, Reuben, Simeon, and Gad, with 151,450 under the standard of Cherub. On the West side, Ephraim, Manasseh and Benjamin, with 108,100, under the standard of the Man. On the North, Dan, Asher and Naphtali, with 157,600 under the standard of the Flying Eagle_" (BP3 129.2-5). Crucially: "_Levi and Joseph are not with the men of war.... Manasseh and Ephraim are substituted for Joseph and Levi, to complete the twelve tribes_" (BP3 129.7, 15). The Rev 7 list inverts this OT substitution (Joseph and Levi re-enter; Manasseh and Dan-and-Ephraim drop) — a deliberate symbolic restructuring.

modernReadings.html: **Same identification — twelve tribes = symbolic spiritual-Israel organization, with the same Dan-and-Ephraim omission noted and the same Juda-heads-the-list emphasis (Lion of Judah).** Bohr develops the omissions as marking the **departed-from-faithfulness** tribes (Dan = idolatry of Jeroboam; Ephraim = idolatry of Israel's northern kingdom; cf. Hos 4:17 — "_Ephraim is joined to idols_").

verses.text: Of the tribe of Aser were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Nepthalim were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Manasses were sealed twelve thousand.

pioneerReadings.html: Continuation of the symbolic-twelve-tribes roll-call (Smith treats vv. 5-8 as a single block).

Continuation of the camp-of-Israel typology Bates laid out for v.5 (BP3 129.2-15). Asher and Naphtali appear on the north side of the wilderness camp under the standard of the Flying Eagle (BP3 129.5); Manasseh is one of the two tribes substituted for Joseph and Levi in the original wilderness organization (BP3 129.15) — but in Rev 7:6 Manasseh is named directly, while Levi is restored at v.7 and Joseph at v.8, an inversion of the OT pattern that flags Rev 7's tribal list as deliberately symbolic.

modernReadings.html: Continuation of the symbolic roll-call (Bohr also treats vv. 5-8 as a block).

verses.text: Of the tribe of Simeon were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Levi were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Issachar were sealed twelve thousand.

pioneerReadings.html: Continuation of the symbolic roll-call. Note Levi included (where in the OT land-allotment Levi was not counted among the twelve land-receiving tribes), filling the slot vacated by the omission of Dan.

Continuation of the typology — Levi's inclusion in the Rev 7:7 list (where Numbers 1-2 had separated Levi for the priesthood and the burden-bearing service of the sanctuary) is the key inversion. Bates notes: "_The tribe of Levi are separated for the Priesthood and burden bearers of all the holy and most holy things. The three sons of Levi with their tribes are stationed as near the Ark of the Testimony as possible, that no wrath come upon the congregation of Israel while they keep the charge of the Covenant (ten Commandments.) Numbers 1:53_" (BP3 129.8-9). In Rev 7 Levi is brought back among the sealed twelve — symbolizing that in the antitype the entire 144,000 are priests-near-the-ark (cf. 1 Pet 2:9, Rev 1:6, 5:10).

modernReadings.html: Continuation of the symbolic roll-call. Bohr also notes Levi's inclusion (replacing Dan) as part of the symbolic restructuring.

verses.text: Of the tribe of Zabulon were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Joseph were sealed twelve thousand. Of the tribe of Benjamin were sealed twelve thousand.

pioneerReadings.html: Conclusion of the symbolic roll-call. Joseph included (where normally the OT roll-calls list Ephraim + Manasseh as the two Joseph-tribes), filling the slot vacated by the omission of Ephraim.

Conclusion of the typology — Joseph's inclusion in Rev 7:8 (where the OT camp organization listed Ephraim and Manasseh as the two Joseph-tribes) is the second key inversion. "_Manasseh and Ephraim are substituted for Joseph and Levi, to complete the twelve tribes. Genesis 48:6_" (BP3 129.15). In Rev 7 the substitution runs backward — Joseph is restored, Ephraim is omitted, Manasseh is retained alone — flagging the list as deliberately symbolic rather than literal-genealogical. Bates' Numbers-2 typology supplies the OT structural background that Smith later uses to argue the omissions-of-Dan-and-Ephraim are deliberate moral markers, not accidents of transmission.

modernReadings.html: Conclusion of the symbolic roll-call. Bohr also notes Joseph's inclusion (replacing Ephraim).

verses.text: After this I beheld, and, lo, a great multitude, which no man could number, of all nations, and kindreds, and people, and tongues, stood before the throne, and before the Lamb, clothed with white robes, and palms in their hands;

pioneerReadings.html: **Convergent with Smith — the great multitude is the redeemed-host gathered before the throne; the 144,000 are the special class of last-generation faithful who pass through the time of trouble alive.** "_[Revelation 7:9-17 quoted.] Those whom the Lamb shall lead by the fountains of living waters, and from whose eyes He shall wipe away all tears, will be those now receiving the knowledge and understanding revealed in the Bible, the Word of God_" (7BC 970.8 — "Strive to Be Among the 144,000"). In GC 648.3 EGW carefully distinguishes the 144,000 ("_translated from the earth, from among the living_") as a sub-class within the great-multitude singing the song of Moses and the Lamb — the same Smith-class arrangement that Bohr softly collapses.

**Great multitude = the full redeemed-host of all ages, drawn from every nation/kindred/people/tongue, standing before the throne in the post-second-advent state.** "**This innumerable multitude is the company of the redeemed of all ages — all whom God has saved from the beginning to the end. The 144,000, sealed and translated alive at the second coming, are a special class within this larger multitude — the last-generation faithful who pass through the time of trouble without seeing death and are translated at the second coming. The white robes are the imputed righteousness of Christ; the palms are the emblems of victory in the conflict won**" (DAR 448.2-449.1, condensed paraphrase).

**Divergent from Smith on framing — Miller reads the great multitude as the END-OF-GENTILE-DISPENSATION harvest, paralleling (but distinct from) the end-of-Jewish-dispensation 144,000.** "_As these were sealed at the close of that dispensation, so John now saw in vision a great number, which no man could number, sealed at the close of the Gentile dispensation, of which he has been prophesying_" (MWV2 187.1). On Miller's two-harvests scheme the 144,000 are the past Jewish harvest and the great multitude is the still-future complete Christian harvest at the second coming. The later Adventist reading (Smith, EGW, Haskell) keeps both groups in the end-time but distinguishes them by mode of redemption (translated alive vs. raised from all ages).

**Convergent with Smith — the great multitude is the redeemed of all ages drawn from every nation; the 144,000 are a special little-company sub-class within it, marked by their unique end-time experience.** "_The creatures of God's love were gathered from all ages. An innumerable company of the redeemed were seen standing before the throne and before the Lamb. They were clothed with the robes of Christ's righteousness; but throughout eternity, they will remember that both robes and palms are the result of the sacrifice of the Son of God_" (SSP 139.1). The 144,000 / great-multitude relation is then framed: "_Then, as if again to call attention to the little company who have suffered most, one elder, pointing to the one hundred and forty-four thousand, said, 'What are these...?'_" (SSP 139.2). For Haskell as for Smith, the 144,000 are a distinct sub-class — "_the little company who have suffered most_" — within the larger multitude.

modernReadings.html: **Great multitude = the redeemed-host, drawn from every nation/kindred/people/tongue, standing before the throne.** Bohr's identification differs softly from Smith on the **relation of the 144,000 to the great multitude**: where Smith reads the 144,000 as a **distinct class** within the larger multitude (specifically the last-generation faithful translated alive at the second advent), Bohr collapses the two groups so that "_the 144,000 of Rev 7:4-8 and the great multitude of Rev 7:9-17 are the SAME group of end-time saints viewed from two different angles — the 144,000 as the numbered, militarily-organized army of God on earth (the Numbers-1-and-2 muster-roll language is in the background), and the great multitude as the same group viewed in their post-translation glorified state standing victorious before the throne_" (pp. 304-308, paraphrasing Stefanovic). The white robes (Bohr: imputed righteousness of Christ + the white-linen of the priesthood) and the palms (Bohr: victory + the Feast-of-Tabernacles imagery of Lev 23:40-43 / Neh 8:14-17, the harvest-home celebration of God's redeemed) are convergent with Smith on the principal symbology.

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: No violation. The 144,000-equals-great-multitude collapse is a soft refinement (not a redefinition); both readings agree on the principal identifications (spiritual Israel, end-time generation, Sabbath-sealing, white-robes / palms emblems).

symbols.label: Symbols redefined
symbols.html: 144,000 / great multitude relation (Smith: 144,000 are a distinct class within the larger multitude — the last-generation faithful translated alive at the second advent; Bohr: 144,000 and great multitude are the same group viewed in two perspectives — pre-translation organized army on earth + post-translation glorified state in heaven).

verses.text: And cried with a loud voice, saying, Salvation to our God which sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb.

pioneerReadings.html: The redeemed-host's doxology ascribing salvation to **the Father (on the throne) AND the Lamb (Christ)** — the same two-person worship object as Rev 5:13.

modernReadings.html: Same identification — the doxology is to **the Father + the Lamb**, the same two-person worship object as Rev 5:13; convergent with Smith.

verses.text: And all the angels stood round about the throne, and about the elders and the four beasts, and fell before the throne on their faces, and worshipped God,

pioneerReadings.html: The angels join the redeemed-host's worship — the three-concentric-circles geometry of Rev 4-5 (Father-on-throne / inner circle of living creatures and elders / outer circle of angelic hosts) is preserved.

**Convergent with Smith on the angelic-worship concentric geometry.** The angelic-host "_acquainted with the experience of the redeemed_" join the song; "_the redeemed host sing the song of their experience_" (SSP 139.1). Haskell describes the harp-and-song circle: "_Music, such as mortal ear never heard, comes from those harps swept by the hands of the redeemed.... Those who have known Him best will bring forth the clearest notes from the instruments, and each chord will tell the history of their lives_" (SSP 251.1). The 144,000's song before the throne and the four beasts and the twenty-four elders (Rev 14:3) is the same song framework (SSP 251.2).

modernReadings.html: Same identification — angels (the outer concentric circle) + the 24 elders + the seraphim (the inner concentric circle) all join in the worship of the Father and the Lamb; convergent with Smith on the three-concentric-circles geometry.

verses.text: Saying, Amen: Blessing, and glory, and wisdom, and thanksgiving, and honour, and power, and might, be unto our God for ever and ever. Amen.

pioneerReadings.html: The angelic doxology of seven attributes (blessing, glory, wisdom, thanksgiving, honor, power, might) — the perfect-completeness number ascribed to God in the consummation-worship.

modernReadings.html: Same identification — the seven-attribute doxology expresses perfect-completeness worship of God; convergent.

verses.text: And one of the elders answered, saying unto me, What are these which are arrayed in white robes? and whence came they?

pioneerReadings.html: **Convergent with Smith on the elder's question prompting the v.14 identification.** EGW's brief gloss focuses on the wedding-garment side of the identification: "_Remember that every one who shall be found with the wedding garment on will have come out of great tribulation_" (5BC 1097.6 / RH April 17, 1894). The elder's question is treated as the narrative pivot prompting the great-tribulation answer, not as the locus of a doctrinal point about WHO the elders are (cf. Rev 4:4).

One of the 24 elders questions John, prompting the v.14 identification of the great multitude. Smith reads this as confirming that the elders are **distinct** from the great multitude (since one of them asks John about the multitude's identity) — but **not** that the elders are non-human (Smith's reading is that the elders are the Matt-27 firstfruits raised at Christ's resurrection, a distinct class from the great multitude of all ages of the redeemed).

**Convergent with Smith — the elder's question is a narrative spotlight on the little-company class within the larger multitude.** "_Then, as if again to call attention to the little company who have suffered most, one elder, pointing to the one hundred and forty-four thousand, said, 'What are these which are arrayed in white robes? and whence came they?'_" (SSP 139.2). For Haskell the elder is pointing TO the 144,000 specifically (not to the whole multitude); the answer in v.14 ("_came out of the great tribulation_") then identifies the 144,000 as the specific end-time class within the larger great multitude — Smith's reading exactly.

modernReadings.html: One of the 24 elders questions John, prompting the v.14 identification. Bohr reads this as confirming that **the elders are non-human** (since one of them asks John about the multitude's identity — they cannot themselves be members of the multitude they are asking about). This is the same divergence carried over from Rev 4:4 (Smith: elders = redeemed humans of Matt-27 firstfruits class; Bohr: elders = angelic representatives of the unfallen worlds).

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: No new violation at v.13; the v.4-of-Rev-4 violation carries over. Smith's reading accommodates the v.13 question without contradiction (the Matt-27 firstfruits class can ask John about the larger great multitude of which they are not the same sub-class), so the question-from-an-elder datum is **not decisive** between the two readings.

verses.text: And I said unto him, Sir, thou knowest. And he said to me, These are they which came out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.

pioneerReadings.html: **Convergent with Smith — "THE great tribulation" is specifically the Dan-12:1 time of trouble of the final crisis, through which the 144,000 pass; the white robes are the wedding garment, washed in the blood of the Lamb.** "_'These are they which came out of great tribulation;' they have passed through the time of trouble such as never was since there was a nation; they have endured the anguish of the time of Jacob's trouble; they have stood without an intercessor through the final outpouring of God's judgments. But they have been delivered, for they have 'washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb'_" (GC 648.3). The Dan-12:1 identification is explicit. The wedding-garment identification: "_Every one who shall be found with the wedding garment on will have come out of great tribulation_" (5BC 1097.6).

**The great multitude = those who came out of the GREAT tribulation (Greek: tēs thlipseōs tēs megalēs, "THE tribulation, THE great-one" — the definite article indicates the specific great tribulation, identified by Smith as the time of trouble of Daniel 12:1 immediately preceding the second advent).** "**The great tribulation here is the time of trouble of Daniel 12:1, the great time of trouble such as never was since there was a nation. The 144,000 are the ones who come up out of this last great tribulation — the rest of the multitude having come out of earlier persecutions and tribulations of every age, all alike washing their robes in the blood of the Lamb**" (DAR 450.1-451.1, paraphrase). Note Smith's reading: **the GREAT tribulation specifically refers to the Dan-12:1 final time of trouble**, and the 144,000 are the ones who come through it; the larger multitude includes the redeemed of every age, each having come through their own period's tribulations.

**Convergent with Smith on the principal v.14 identifications (great tribulation, white robes washed in the blood of the Lamb), but with Miller's two-harvest scheme attached.** "_'And he said unto me, These are they which come out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.' This passage shows who those were that John saw, and how they obtained the honor and glory, which John saw them possessing, through great tribulation, and the blood of the Lamb_" (MWV2 187.2). For Miller the great-tribulation answer applies to the entire end-of-Gentile-dispensation great multitude (since the 144,000 are already past); for the later Adventists the great-tribulation is specifically the Dan-12:1 final-crisis through which the 144,000 (a sub-class within the multitude) pass.

**Convergent with Smith — the great tribulation is specifically the end-time tribulation through which the 144,000 (the last-generation little company) pass; their unique character is that they have stood without a mediator and developed perfect characters in a degenerate age.** "_The place once occupied by Satan and his angels, will be filled by the hundred and forty-four thousand, when they gather at last about the throne, where they serve God day and night in His temple_" (SSP 140.1). "_These are redeemed from among men. They come from the last generation,—that race which is almost extinct because of the prevalence of disease and sin. But the blood of the Lamb is all powerful, and places these next the throne_" (SSP 141.1). "_In their mouth was found no guile; for the soul temple had been so thoroughly cleansed before leaving earth that the human mouth became a channel for the words of God_" (SSP 250.1).

modernReadings.html: **The great multitude = the saints who come out of "THE great tribulation" of the end-time, identified by Bohr as the same final-crisis period (the time of trouble of Dan 12:1 + the seven last plagues of Rev 16); convergent with Smith on the THE-great-tribulation identification.** Bohr's 144,000-equals-great-multitude collapse means he reads the entire great multitude as the end-time saints (rather than as the redeemed of all ages with the 144,000 as a sub-class), but the principal identification of the GREAT tribulation as the end-time final crisis is **the same** as Smith's.

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: No violation per se on v.14. The Rev 7:9 soft refinement on the 144,000 / great multitude relation carries over.

verses.text: Therefore are they before the throne of God, and serve him day and night in his temple: and he that sitteth on the throne shall dwell among them.

pioneerReadings.html: **Convergent with Smith — the redeemed serve God day and night in his temple in unbroken eternal-state worship.** "_'Therefore are they before the throne of God, and serve Him day and night in His temple: and He that sitteth on the throne shall dwell among them'_" (GC 648.3, quoting the verse as the consummation of the 144,000's experience). The unmediated dwelling-with-God language echoes Lev 26:11-12, Ezek 37:27, Rev 21:3 — the consummation of the covenant promise.

The redeemed serve God day and night in his temple — the eternal-state worship in the new-earth temple (where, as Rev 21-22 reveal, the temple is the Lord God Almighty and the Lamb themselves, dwelling unmediated among the redeemed). Smith's general framework: this is the eternal-state vision of the redeemed serving in unbroken worship.

**Convergent with Smith — eternal-state worship around the throne.** "_The place once occupied by Satan and his angels, will be filled by the hundred and forty-four thousand, when they gather at last about the throne, where they serve God day and night in His temple, with God Himself dwelling in their midst. This is their reward for the hunger and thirst endured on earth. They form the bodyguard of their Saviour, and He leads them to the fountain of living waters_" (SSP 140.1). Haskell adds the bodyguard-of-the-Saviour identification (drawn from Rev 14:4 "_follow the Lamb whithersoever he goeth_") as the specific eternal role of the 144,000 within the larger redeemed-host.

modernReadings.html: Same identification — eternal-state worship, with the temple as the unmediated presence of God dwelling among his people (cross-link to Rev 21:3, 22 + Ezek 37:27 — "_my tabernacle also shall be with them: yea, I will be their God, and they shall be my people_"); convergent with Smith.

verses.text: They shall hunger no more, neither thirst any more; neither shall the sun light on them, nor any heat.

pioneerReadings.html: **Convergent with Smith — the eternal-state reversal of hunger, thirst, scorching heat (cross-link to Isa 49:10).** "_They have seen the earth wasted with famine and pestilence, the sun having power to scorch men with great heat, and they themselves have endured suffering, hunger, and thirst. But 'they shall hunger no more, neither thirst any more; neither shall the sun light on them, nor any heat'_" (GC 648.3). The verses move directly from the time-of-trouble hardships (the 144,000's earthly experience) to the eternal reversal. EGW also flags that many of the mysteries woven into the eternal-state promise will only be unfolded in heaven (6BC 1091.6).

The eternal-state promise: no more hunger, thirst, scorching sun — the reversal of the curse and the fulfillment of Isa 49:10 ("_They shall not hunger nor thirst; neither shall the heat nor sun smite them_"). Smith reads this as the consummation-promise of the eternal state.

modernReadings.html: Same identification — eternal-state promise, with the explicit Isa-49:10 cross-link as the controlling Old Testament parallel; convergent with Smith. Exemplary Rule V (the language is verbatim Isaiah's).

verses.text: For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them, and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters: and God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes.

pioneerReadings.html: **Convergent with Smith — the Lamb-as-Shepherd in the midst of the throne leads the redeemed to living-waters fountains; God wipes away all tears (cross-link to Isa 25:8, Psa 23, Rev 21:4).** "_'For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them, and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters: and God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes'_" (GC 648.3, climax of the 144,000-passage). The Christological consummation: the Lamb who was slain (Rev 5:6) is now the Shepherd-King leading the redeemed in unmediated pasture (cf. Rev 22:1-5). Many of the unfolding-mysteries of the eternal state are reserved for heaven's school (6BC 1091.6).

The Lamb-as-Shepherd in the midst of the throne — the Christological consummation in which the redeemed are forever cared for by the Lamb, and God himself wipes away all tears (cross-link to Isa 25:8 + Rev 21:4). The eternal-state pastoral imagery (Lamb feeds them, leads them to living-waters fountains, wipes away tears).

**Convergent with Smith — Lamb-as-Shepherd leads the 144,000 to living-waters fountains as their special eternal reward.** "_They form the bodyguard of their Saviour, and He leads them to the fountain of living waters. They, who, on earth clung to the knowledge of_ [God in spite of suffering, now drink of the unmediated fountain]" (SSP 140.1). The Lamb-as-Shepherd pastoral imagery is the climax of the 144,000's experience — the reversal of the hunger-and-thirst-and-scorching-heat of v.16.

modernReadings.html: Same identification — Lamb-as-Shepherd in the eternal state, with the same Isa 25:8 + Rev 21:4 + Psa 23 cross-links; convergent with Smith.

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: No violation. Convergent. Exemplary Rule-V cross-links (Isa 25:8 + Psa 23 + Rev 21:4).

summaryHtml:
**Rev 7 violation summary:** Of 17 verses, Bohr has **zero hard Miller-rule violations**. The chapter is the **cleanest convergence** in the entire Bohr-vs.-Smith audit so far. Convergent on every major identification: four winds = destroying judgments; four angels = providential restrainers; sealing angel from the east = the great end-time Sabbath-sealing message; seal of the living God = the Sabbath of the fourth commandment; 144,000 = spiritual Israel of the last generation (not literal Jewish lineage); twelve tribes = symbolic gospel-grafting organization (Rom 11 / Gal 3:29 framework, with Dan and Ephraim omitted for their OT idolatry); great multitude = the redeemed-host before the throne, drawn from every nation/kindred/tongue/people; THE great tribulation = the end-time Dan-12:1 time of trouble; eternal-state pastoral consummation with Lamb-as-Shepherd. The **one soft refinement** (not violation) is at v.9: Bohr collapses the 144,000 into the great multitude as the same end-time saints in two perspectives, where Smith reads the 144,000 as a distinct class within the larger multitude. Both readings agree on the spiritual-Israel / end-time-generation / Sabbath-test framework. The Rev-4:4 elders-as-angels divergence carries over at v.13 (one-of-the-elders questions John), but is not a new violation.

---
